title: AT&T Fiber, HBO Max Upsell
alt_title: AT&T Fiber with HBO Max Included
body: Developed responsive HTML email template for AT&T Fiber's HBO Max bundle campaign promoting entertainment-grade internet with included streaming benefits. Built promotional email featuring animated GIF hero image with visual motion effects showcasing AT&T Fiber and HBO Max co-branding.

Implemented multi-section informational layout highlighting 1 GIG internet speeds starting at $39.99/month, 1000Mbps connection capabilities, and unlimited data ($30 value) bundled with HBO Max access. Coded detailed service comparison messaging emphasizing bandwidth advantages over cable providers (Xfinity, Spectrum, Cox) with supporting footnotes and disclaimers. Developed mobile-responsive design with optimized image performance, gradient CTAs, and featured content grid showcasing Westworld and Friends programming to drive fiber upgrade conversions.
desc: Promotional email for AT&T Fiber gigabit internet now bundled with HBO Max at $39.99/mo.

title: Aimmune Palforzia Patient Journey Email
alt_title: Palforzia Peanut Allergy Trial Inspiration Email
body: Developed responsive HTML email template for Aimmune Therapeutics' Palforzia pharmaceutical marketing campaign, targeting families enrolled in peanut allergy treatment programs. Built patient journey email featuring testimonial video content and educational resources including prescribing information, medication guides, and safety documentation links.

Implemented personalized for patient messaging while ensuring strict pharmaceutical compliance and accessibility standards with comprehensive alt-text attributes. Optimized image-to-text ratios for enhanced deliverability and tested mobile-responsive layout with image swap functionality across all major email clients. Maintained regulatory-compliant footer elements including privacy notices, unsubscribe mechanisms, and required pharmaceutical disclosures for HIPAA-sensitive patient communications.
desc: Aimmune Therapeutics email encouraging Palforzia peanut allergy patients to keep going, with a patient family testimonial video.

title: DIRECTV and NBC partnership
alt_title: DirecTV Bluff City Law Premiere (NBC)
body: This project was a static promotional email for the DIRECTV and NBC partnership, built to showcase network content with a strong, image forward layout while staying dependable across inboxes. I handled the build end to end, translating the creative into a responsive template that preserved the visual impact on desktop and mobile, while also meeting accessibility requirements through proper alt text and clear reading order.

The email was tuned for performance and deliverability by optimizing image assets, keeping a healthy image to text balance, and using mobile image swaps where needed. It was tested across major email clients and devices to ensure consistent rendering, resulting in a lightweight, standards compliant template that balanced branding, accessibility, and reliability.
desc: DirecTV email promoting the premiere of NBC's legal drama Bluff City Law starring Jimmy Smits and Caitlin McGee, airing Mondays.

title: AT&T iPhone XS Promotional Email
alt_title: AT&T iPhone XS 50% Off Upgrade
body: Developed responsive HTML email template in Salesforce Marketing Cloud for AT&T's iPhone XS campaign featuring 50% device discount ($450 savings) with personalized targeting. Implemented AMPscript dynamic personalization for customer names and device eligibility criteria. Built multi-section layout showcasing network modernization messaging, 3G phase-out communication, compatibility tools, and trade-in CTAs.

Coded promotional hierarchy with product imagery, savings calculations, and qualifying legal disclaimers. Optimized mobile-responsive design with gradient buttons, icon-based features, and cross-client compatibility. Ensured accessibility with comprehensive alt-text and CAN-SPAM compliant footer.
desc: AT&T customer email offering 50% off the iPhone XS with no trade-in, tied to the 3G network sunset.

title: AT&T Service Message
alt_title: AT&T DirecTV Billing Credit Notice
body: Developed responsive HTML email template in Salesforce Marketing Cloud for AT&T's customer service communication addressing billing errors and account credits. Built transactional email with dynamic personalization logic using AMPscript to display customer names, credit amounts, and account-specific billing details while maintaining a clear, empathetic tone for service recovery messaging.

Implemented clean, accessible layout optimized for readability with prominent contact information and support links. Ensured cross-client compatibility and mobile responsiveness for critical account notifications, following transactional email best practices with CAN-SPAM compliant footer elements and clear customer support pathways to build trust during service issue resolution.
desc: AT&T service email notifying a DirecTV customer of a one-time credit for a DVR service double-charge.

title: AT&T direct marketing email
alt_title: DirecTV The Titan Games Premiere
body: Developed responsive HTML email template in for AT&T's DirecTV win-back campaign partnering with NBC to promote "The Titan Games" premiere. Collaborated with marketing teams to create promotional content targeting lapsed DirecTV subscribers with exclusive programming offers.

Implemented CAN-SPAM compliant footer with unsubscribe links and mailing address, accessibility-focused alt-text attributes, and optimized image-to-text ratios for deliverability. Built mobile-responsive layout with social media integration and dynamic account linking, testing rendering across major email clients to drive re-engagement and tune-in rates.
desc: DirecTV email promoting the NBC series The Titan Games with Dwayne Johnson premiering on the network.

title: DirecTV Television Content Upsell Email
alt_title: DirecTV Switch to SELECT Package Savings Email

Built promotional layout featuring "YouMeHer" programming imagery and value proposition messaging encouraging package downgrades to eliminate unused sports network costs like NBC Sports Network.

Implemented mobile-responsive design with optimized image compression for faster load times and improved deliverability through strategic image-to-text ratio management. Ensured ADA accessibility compliance with comprehensive alt-text attributes for all imagery including hero graphics and product screenshots. Coded clean HTML structure with cross-client compatibility testing across major email platforms and maintained CAN-SPAM compliant footer with subscription management and DirecTV service details.

Dynamic AT&T service message template

It was optimized for fast load times, strong deliverability, and accessibility, making it a reusable foundation for high volume service communications.

title: The Podcast Fellowship
alt_title: The Podcast Fellowship Landing Page
body: Product Manager

I've been the fractional Technical Product Manager for The Podcast Fellowship since 2021. TPF is a non-profit that pays students to listen to educational podcasts, pretty cool mission. I oversee their offshore dev team and manage the web application that runs the whole program.

My role is different from my usual email marketing and CRM work. I handle technical architecture decisions, prioritize the product roadmap, and translate between the non-technical nonprofit leadership and the development team. I guide frontend development, optimize user experience, and make sure the platform stays reliable as the program grows. It's been a great experience managing a consumer web app outside my typical marketing technology wheelhouse.
desc: Landing page for The Podcast Fellowship, a program that pays participants to complete and share podcasts.

title: Yogaglo website and application
alt_title: Glo App Login Page
body: From 2016 - 2108, I was a front-end developer at Glo, and developed the UI for YogaGlo's web application, a full-stack platform built with Ember.js and Express.
Back in 2016, streaming exercise classes online was cutting edge!

I worked on multiple features including the login page highlighting their "Notes" feature, user dashboard, and class browsing interface. Built responsive, mobile-first interfaces using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that performed consistently across all devices and browsers. Focused on creating an intuitive user experience for yoga practitioners to access classes, save personal notes, and filter content by their preferences.
desc: Login landing page for the Glo yoga and fitness app promoting a new class notes feature.

title: American Express Financial Calculators - Auto Loan
alt_title: Amex Credit Intel Home Equity Loan Calculator
body: I was the lead developer across a suite of about seven interactive financial calculators for American Express Credit Intel, including the Auto Loan Calculator. I built these tools with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, focusing on fast, responsive UI, accessible form controls, and a consistent interaction pattern so users could quickly understand outcomes without friction across desktop and mobile.

For the Auto Loan Calculator specifically, I implemented the core amortization and payment logic and wired it to a clean input experience with strong validation and guardrails. That included handling common edge cases like zero down payment, short terms, very low or high APRs, and rounding consistency so the monthly payment, total interest, and overall cost stay accurate and stable as users adjust inputs.
desc: Interactive American Express Credit Intel tool estimating how much a homeowner may be eligible to borrow.

title: American Express Partnership Landing Page
alt_title: Amex Hilton Honors Card Redesign
body: This project was a mobile-first partnership landing page for American Express and Hilton Honors, designed to explain card benefits and guide users through a card design update without disrupting their experience. I built the page as a fully responsive layout, translating a dense set of product details, FAQs, and support content into a clear, scannable flow that worked equally well on desktop and mobile. The goal was to reduce confusion while keeping the messaging aligned with both brands.

On the technical side, I focused on responsive layout, performance, and cross-browser consistency using modern HTML, CSS, and grid-based layouts. I tested the page across common devices and browsers to make sure typography, spacing, and content stacking behaved predictably. The result was a flexible, maintainable landing page that could support ongoing content updates without breaking the user experience.
desc: American Express landing page announcing the redesigned Hilton Honors Card and its ongoing benefits.

title: Email for coding school
alt_title: Code Lab Coding Lessons Reminder
body: This project was a fully custom marketing and progress reminder email for a coding school, designed and built around 2018 to encourage students to complete upcoming lessons. I handled both the visual design and the front end email development, creating all of the assets in Photoshop and translating the layouts into responsive, email-safe HTML and CSS. The content combined personalized messaging with course tiles and clear calls to action, which was a fairly modern pattern for education emails at the time.

From a technical perspective, the focus was on reliable rendering across the major email clients and devices that were common in that era. I tested and refined the build to account for client limitations, image handling, and layout fallbacks. The result was a cohesive, on-brand template that balanced personalization, usability, and the constraints of email in 2018.
desc: Code Lab email reminding a student of upcoming coding lessons to complete before earning certification.

alt_title: Petite 'n Pretty Glitter Beauty Landing Page

Implemented reusable Liquid templates and modular components to enable consistent brand styling across product pages and marketing content. Focused on creating a flexible, maintainable codebase with responsive design patterns that allowed the Petite-n-Pretty team to scale their e-commerce platform while maintaining visual consistency and performance across all devices.
desc: Petite 'n Pretty kids' cosmetics page featuring Gen Glitter, 10K Shine Lip Gloss and 9021-GLOW! palette.

The result was a lightweight, reusable template the marketing team could rely on for future feature announcements.

title: Ozempic Email
alt_title: Ozempic Cardiovascular Outcomes HCP Email
body: This project involved building a highly regulated pharmaceutical email for Ozempic focused on presenting CVOT study data and key safety information to a clinical audience. I handled the full front end email build, translating a dense, data-heavy design into responsive, email-safe HTML and CSS that could reliably render across major clients. The layout needed to balance marketing content with mandatory prescribing and safety sections, while keeping the hierarchy clear and the reading experience usable for busy healthcare professionals.

On the technical side, the work emphasized strict compliance and QA, including CAN-SPAM considerations, accessibility best practices like proper alt text, and defensive coding for inconsistent client behavior. I tested the template using Litmus and Email on Acid to validate rendering, spacing, and typography across devices and inboxes. The final result was a compliant, production-ready template that could support complex medical content without sacrificing reliability or readability.
desc: Novo Nordisk healthcare-provider email presenting Ozempic CVOT data for patients with T2D and established CVD.

title: AT&T FirstNet First Responder Email
alt_title: AT&T FirstNet First Responder Offer
body: Developed responsive HTML email template for AT&T's FirstNet B2B marketing campaign targeting first responders and public safety entities with dedicated wireless network migration offers. Built promotional email featuring $50 billion credit incentive messaging, service benefit highlights, and eligibility verification CTAs for emergency services personnel.

Implemented personalized name variables and coded multi-section informational layout showcasing FirstNet's priority access, security, and reliability features with icon-based design elements. Optimized mobile-responsive design with image-to-text ratio management for deliverability and ensured cross-client compatibility. Maintained accessibility compliance with comprehensive alt-text and structured detailed eligibility footnotes for complex B2B government offer terms.
desc: AT&T email offering a $50 bill credit to first responders who migrate their line to the FirstNet network.

title: Emory Healthcare - Custom-Built SFMC Calendar
alt_title: Emory Healthcare SFMC Email Calendar Tool
body: Built with: Salesforce Marketing Cloud, custom Cloud Pages, SSJS, AMPscript, SQL (Query Activities), and the SFMC REST and SOAP APIs.

I built an internal calendar app that gives the marketing team a single view of every email and journey going out across the health system. Before this, there was no easy way to see what was scheduled, what overlapped, or which patients were getting hit by multiple campaigns at once. Now it all lives on one screen.

It runs entirely inside Salesforce Marketing Cloud as a custom Cloud Page. I designed the whole data pipeline: nightly automations pull journey and campaign data into cached data extensions, and a one click Sync refreshes it on demand. A separate admin page lets the team control how everything displays (service lines, color coding, categories, manual campaigns, exclusions) without touching any code.

The most interesting part was the overlap and saturation logic: figuring out, across thousands of sends, which patients were receiving too many messages in a given window, and surfacing that clearly on the calendar. I also wired in campaign metrics, so the team can click any day and see how a campaign actually performed on opens, clicks, and sends.
desc: Emory Healthcare Salesforce Marketing Cloud email calendar dashboard showing scheduled sends, categories, and weekly overlaps.

title: Toyota U.S. Figure Skating Partnership (Alysa Liu)
alt_title: Toyota U.S. Figure Skating Partnership (Alysa Liu)

Toyota Olympics campaign (Figure Skating version)

title: American Express - Shop Small -Email 1
alt_title: Amex Shop Small Business Saturday Toolkit
body: Developed technically complex responsive HTML email template in Salesforce Marketing Cloud for American Express's Shop Small holiday marketing campaign. Implemented advanced VML techniques to create background images behind live text for Outlook compatibility, ensuring visual consistency across email clients with varying CSS support. Built multi-section layout featuring Shop Small Map enrollment CTAs, Door to Shop Small AR experience promotion, and holiday resource content with dynamic merchant personalization using AMPscript.

Employed sophisticated responsive design with detailed media queries and breakpoints to maintain precise alignment and spacing across devices. Integrated custom conditional logic for cross-client compatibility while managing interactive elements and ensuring accessibility compliance throughout the complex merchant-focused campaign.
desc: American Express merchant email with Small Business Saturday tips, Shop Small map listing, and free marketing materials.

title: Brilliant Earth - Abandoned Cart Analysis
alt_title: Brilliant Earth Cart Abandonment Email
body: This project focused on clarifying the business rules behind the entire campaign. I worked through how the system decides who qualifies, what counts as abandonment, how session timing affects triggers, and how purchases or recent messages suppress sends. While the creative supported dynamic products and pricing, the core work was making sure the rules reflected real shopping behavior and matched stakeholder expectations.

On the technical and strategy side, we pressure tested edge cases like multiple cart events in a day, switching products, and overlaps with other campaigns. We clarified when a series should restart, when a newer cart should replace an older one, and when a customer should be suppressed entirely. The result was a scalable, frequency safe campaign with clear, documented logic for who gets the message, which product appears, and when it should or should not send.
desc: Brilliant Earth abandoned-cart email showing a saved diamond ring with product recommendations and a free-gift offer.

title: Aimmune Palforzia Healthcare Provider Email
alt_title: Palforzia Peanut Allergy HCP Email
body: Developed responsive HTML email template for Aimmune Therapeutics targeting healthcare providers with Palforzia prescribing information and clinical guidance. Optimized for HCP segmentation while maintaining pharmaceutical standards for image-to-text ratios and deliverability. Ensured cross-client compatibility across medical professional email systems with mobile-responsive design supporting clinical workflows.
desc: Aimmune Therapeutics healthcare-provider email introducing Palforzia oral immunotherapy for pediatric peanut allergy.

title: Emory Healthcare - Patient Reengagement Campaign
alt_title: Patient Reengagement Email Journey Map
body: Built with: Salesforce Marketing Cloud, SQL (Query Activities), Journey Builder, SSJS, AMPscript, custom Cloud Pages, and the SFMC REST and SOAP APIs.

I built an automated campaign to win back lapsed patients, people who had engaged with Emory before but had gone quiet. The goal was to reach them again with something relevant to their care, not just a generic blast.

The core is a data pipeline I designed in SQL: it looks at engagement history, identifies who has lapsed, and rebuilds a fresh audience on a schedule. Each patient is then routed by service line based on the care area of their last interaction, so someone who last saw orthopedics gets different messaging than someone who saw cardiology. From there they flow into a Journey Builder journey with A/B tested emails, so we could actually learn what brings people back rather than guess.

I built the whole thing with a full test harness too: every production piece (the audience, the journey, the emails) has a parallel test version, and the entire flow runs end to end in test before anything reaches a real patient. The screenshot is a data flow page I built to map every step of the pipeline and its status on one screen, so the team can see the whole system at a glance.
desc: A multi-track lifecycle flowchart mapping the trigger logic and send cadence of a patient reengagement email campaign.

title:
United Health Care
Design, Development & QA
alt_title: UnitedHealthcare 'This IS News!' Insurance Solutions Newsletter
body: This project demonstrates my ability to take a basic Word document brief with brand assets and transform it into a fully designed and then coded email. I handled the process end-to-end: interpreting the brief, applying design principles, and developing a responsive, cross-client-compatible HTML email.
Interpreting the Brief
Extracted key messaging and visual hierarchy from the Word document.
Identified essential content sections like the headline, callouts (e.g., Service Heroes), and closing remarks for emphasis.
Design Stage
Visual Layout: Created a clear and engaging layout based on the content, emphasizing headers, quotes, and recognition sections.
Brand Consistency: Applied UnitedHealthcare's brand guidelines, including typography, colors, and image placement.
Mobile-First Approach: Designed with responsive layouts to ensure readability and functionality across devices.
Development Stage
HTML Coding: Hand-coded the email to reflect the design, ensuring clean, maintainable, and responsive code.
Cross-Client Compatibility: Addressed quirks in email clients like Outlook using techniques such as inline styles and VML for background images.
Accessibility Compliance: Added alt text for images and structured the content for screen readers to ensure usability for all audiences.
Quality Assurance
Rendering Tests: Used Email on Acid to validate design consistency across 90+ email clients.
Functionality Tests: Verified all links and interactive elements worked correctly.
Performance Optimization: Minimized load time by compressing assets and cleaning up code.
desc: UnitedHealthcare Insurance Solutions internal newsletter celebrating Service Heroes and Medicare Supplement business wins, shown alongside its Word source copy.

title: Dynamic Email, AT&T and CBS Sports
alt_title: AT&T U-verse CBS Channels Restored Notice
body: This project focused on building a fully dynamic, production ready marketing email for AT&T announcing the return of CBS Sports and Smithsonian content. I handled the full lifecycle, from layout and responsive behavior to data driven personalization and performance tuning.

The template supported dynamic body copy, mobile image swaps, and solid fallbacks across major email clients, with accessibility covered through proper alt text and clear content hierarchy while staying aligned to AT&T’s brand.

On the technical side, the email was optimized for fast load times and reliable inbox placement by managing image size, compression, and image to text balance. It was tested across major clients and devices and structured to plug cleanly into AT&T’s dynamic content pipeline, resulting in a flexible, reusable template that balanced performance, accessibility, and deliverability.

title: T-Mobile HINT Campaign
alt_title: T-Mobile 5G Home Internet Signup
body: I was a campaign manager for T-Mobile’s 5G Home Internet offering - a coordinated six-touch sequence across email and SMS. The campaign was sent to over a million recipients nationwide and was designed to move prospects through awareness, eligibility confirmation, and activation messaging over several weeks.

I acted as the liaison between business stakeholders, marketing, and technical teams, helping translate business requirements into executable campaign logic and keeping timelines, dependencies, and scope aligned across groups.
Beyond coordination, I was closely involved in shaping the overall flow of the program, including message sequencing, timing between touches, and how different channels were orchestrated together.
Email Journeys and Cadence:
Designed structured email journeys (e.g., T1 to T6), balancing engagement frequency with customer experience.
Aligned messaging and timing with customer lifecycle stages to maximize impact.
Campaign Strategy:
Contributed to crafting strategic messaging for email and SMS touchpoints to drive engagement and conversions.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to ensure campaigns met broader marketing objectives.
Data Pipelines:
Oversaw data flow from multiple sources into SFMC, ensuring accurate segmentation and personalization.
Managed tools like Control-M to monitor and maintain pipeline integrity for seamless campaign execution.
KPI Tracking and Optimization:
Monitored key metrics like delivery rate, bounce rate, and engagement to measure campaign success.
Used performance data to refine future strategies and improve ROI.

title: Emory Healthcare - KPI Dashboard
alt_title: Emory Healthcare Email KPI Dashboard
body: Built with: Salesforce Marketing Cloud, custom Cloud Pages, SSJS, AMPscript, SQL (Query Activities), and the SFMC REST and SOAP APIs.

Salesforce Marketing Cloud does have built in reporting, but it's clunky and not something the team wanted to open every day. So I built a clean, simple dashboard on top of the same data: one screen that shows how their email is actually performing (sends, opens, clicks, bounces, and unsubscribes), rolled up by campaign, category, and service line. Instead of digging through native reports, they get one always current view of the whole program.

It runs entirely inside Salesforce Marketing Cloud as a custom Cloud Page. I built the data pipeline behind it: nightly automations aggregate raw send and tracking data into a summary data extension, and a Sync button refreshes the most recent activity on demand. A separate admin page lets the team map campaigns to categories and service lines and manage exclusions, so the rollups stay accurate even as campaign naming changes over time.

The hardest part was making the numbers trustworthy at scale. I reworked the nightly refresh to process data in weekly chunks and switched it to an upsert model, which stopped it from double counting and from failing on high volume days. The frontend is a single Cloud Page I designed for a clean, focused UI, rendered with AMPscript and SSJS, with the interactive metrics, filters, and drilldowns built in vanilla JavaScript, CSS, and HTML.

(Image below has sensitive info redacted)
desc: Interactive email marketing KPI dashboard for Emory Healthcare with filters and journey performance breakdowns.

title: American Express Financial Calculators - Debt Consolidation

body: I was the lead developer across a suite of about seven interactive financial calculators for American Express Credit Intel, including the credit card debt consolidation calculator, a Credit Intel interactive that helps users compare payoff scenarios and understand tradeoffs like interest cost, monthly payment, and timeline. I built the experience end to end with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, focusing on a clean UI, responsive layout, and accessibility so it worked smoothly across devices and was usable with keyboards and screen readers.

The harder part was the math: implementing and validating the financial calculations with careful handling for rounding, input constraints, edge cases, and realistic output formatting that stays consistent as inputs change. I also added guardrails and clear error states to prevent bad inputs, and structured the code to be maintainable as requirements evolved, since these tools tend to get iterative updates once stakeholders see real user behavior.
desc: Three-step American Express Credit Intel tool comparing debt before and after consolidation.
